title: Mapping the crystallization landscape of rare earth MOFs: a high-throughput investigation of structure, kinetics, and selectivity
abstract:

Rapid construction of phase diagrams for rare earth metal–organic framework (MOF) crystallization enabled by high-throughput, automated synthesis and characterization methods.

title: Interfacial effects in Janus HfSSe/SnSSe van der Waals heterostructure: synergistic enhancement of optical absorption and thermoelectric performance
abstract:
Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys., 2026, 28,14621-14631
Ali Shahzad, Santunu Purohit, Lin Zhu
The Janus HfSSe/SnSSe vdW heterostructure exhibits type-II band alignment, facilitating efficient interlayer charge transfer and strong broadband optical absorption, as well as a high Seebeck coefficient and ultralow lattice thermal conductivity.
relevance_reason: DFT calculations on Janus heterostructure for electronic and transport properties, explicit atoms and method application satisfy (a)-(c).

title: Electronic structure and elasticity of the Ta-W solid solution
abstract:
Author(s): Kareem Abdelmaqsoud, John R. Kitchin, and M. Widom
The brittleness or ductility of metals has long been attributed to their elastic constants, with a high Poisson ratio, or equivalently, a high Pugh ratio, favoring greater ductility. Growing evidence links ductility with their electronic structure. Consequently, it is desirable to understand how the…
[Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 053605] Published Tue May 26, 2026
relevance_reason: Computational DFT study of electronic structure and elasticity of Ta-W, satisfying (a)-(c).

title: Superionic Lithium Mobility in Low Symmetry Li7Si2S7I Polymorph Accessed via Si2S7 Dimer Reorientation
abstract:
Chem. Sci., 2026, Accepted Manuscript

We report the experimental discovery of Li7Si2S7I0.89Cl0.11, a triclinic (P-1) analogue of the recently discovered monoclinic (P21/n) Li7Si2S7I (LSSI), which retains the high computationally predicted Li+ ion conductivity of LSSI....
relevance_reason: Experimental discovery of a Li-ion conductor guided by computational predictions (likely DFT/MD) of conductivity; satisfies (a)-(c) with explicit atomistic simulations.
